segments.text: Then Nahash the Ammonite came up, and encamped against Jabesh-gilead:
Make a covenant with us, and we will serve thee.
segments.speaker: Men of Jabesh

segments.text: On this condition will I make a covenant with you, that I may thrust out all your right eyes, and lay it for a reproach upon all Israel.
segments.speaker: Nahash the Ammonite

segments.text: Give us seven days' respite, that we may send messengers unto all the coasts of Israel: and then, if there be no man to save us, we will come out to thee.
segments.speaker: Elders of Jabesh

segments.text: Then came the messengers to Gibeah of Saul, and told the tidings in the ears of the people: and all the people lifted up their voices, and wept.
And, behold, Saul came after the herd out of the field;
What aileth the people that they weep?
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: And they told him the tidings of the men of Jabesh.
And the Spirit of God came upon Saul when he heard those tidings, and his anger was kindled greatly.
And he took a yoke of oxen, and hewed them in pieces, and sent them throughout all the coasts of Israel by the hands of messengers,
Whosoever cometh not forth after Saul and after Samuel, so shall it be done unto his oxen.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: And the fear of the LORD fell on the people, and they came out with one consent.
And when he numbered them in Bezek, the children of Israel were three hundred thousand, and the men of Judah thirty thousand.
Thus shall ye say unto the men of Jabesh-gilead, To morrow, by that time the sun be hot, ye shall have help.
segments.speaker: Messengers

segments.text: And the messengers came and shewed it to the men of Jabesh; and they were glad.
To morrow we will come out unto you, and ye shall do with us all that seemeth good unto you.
segments.speaker: Men of Jabesh

segments.text: And it was so on the morrow, that Saul put the people in three companies; and they came into the midst of the host in the morning watch, and slew the Ammonites until the heat of the day: and it came to pass, that they which remained were scattered, so that two of them were not left together.
Who is he that said, Shall Saul reign over us? bring the men, that we may put them to death.
segments.speaker: People

segments.text: There shall not a man be put to death this day: for to day the LORD hath wrought salvation in Israel.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Come, and let us go to Gilgal, and renew the kingdom there.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: And all the people went to Gilgal; and there they made Saul king before the LORD in Gilgal; and there they sacrificed sacrifices of peace offerings before the LORD; and there Saul and all the men of Israel rejoiced greatly.
